message.content:
In /Users/mkozin/Developer/radiacode-ios (excluding .claude/worktrees):
1. Find all usages of PrecisionAxisValueFormatter — where it's instantiated
2. Find the chart view file that uses it — read its full contents (likely RadiationLevelChartView or similar near /Radiationlevel/Charts/)
3. Also read the file at /RadiaCode/Presentation/Containers/Radiationlevel/Charts/RadiationLevelView/ChartView/ — list files there

Report file paths and relevant code.

attachment.content:
- make: Run the project Makefile (regenerates Xcode project via xcodegen and opens it).
- pr: Wrap current uncommitted changes into a Feature/RAD-XXXX branch and open a Bitbucket PR.
- post-merge: Sync main after a PR has been merged (git checkout main + pull --ff-only + make).
- pdf-viewer:open: Open a PDF in the interactive viewer
- pdf-viewer:fill-form: Fill PDF form fields interactively with live visual feedback
- pdf-viewer:sign: Place a signature or initials image on a PDF
- pdf-viewer:annotate: Collaboratively annotate a PDF — propose markup, review together, iterate
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:extract-design
- pdf-viewer:view-pdf: Interactive PDF viewer. Use when the user wants to open, show, or view a PDF and collaborate on it visually — annotate, highlight, stamp, fill form fields, place signature/initials, or review markup together. Not for summarization or text extraction (use native Read instead).
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review

1	//
2	//  RadiationLevelChartView.swift
3	//  Radiacode
4	//
5	//  Created by Matvey Kozin on 06.02.2024.
6	//
7	
8	import SwiftUI
9	import DGCharts
10	import Combine
11	
12	struct RadiationLevelChartView: UIViewRepresentable {
13	    
14	    let rawData: [RadiationLevelChartDataModel]
15	    let purifiedData: [RadiationLevelChartDataModel]
16	    let timeRange: RadiationLevelOptions.TimeRange
17	    @Binding var scale: RadiationLevelOptions.Scale
18	    
19	    let radiationType: RadiationType.Level
20	    let alarms: Alarm.Level?
21	    let mainSourceForCalculating: Bool
22	    @Binding var indexes: [Int]
23	
24	    func makeUIView(context: Context) -> LineChartView {
25	        let chartView = LineChartView.makeRadiationLevelChart(
26	            rawData: rawData,
27	            purifiedData: purifiedData,
28	            timeRange: timeRange,
29	            scale: scale,
30	            color: NSUIColor(radiationType.accentColor),
31	            coordinator: context.coordinator,
32	            indexes: $indexes,
33	            mainSourceForCalculating: mainSourceForCalculating
34	        )
35	        
36	        drawLines(chart: chartView)
37	        context.coordinator.cachedAlarms = alarms
38	        
39	        return chartView
40	    }
41	    
42	    func updateUIView(_ uiView: LineChartView, context: Context) {
43	        let coordinator = context.coordinator
44	        uiView.updateData(rawData: rawData, purifiedData: purifiedData, coordinator: coordinator)
45	        let updateMoving = uiView.updateMoving(scale: scale, timeRange: timeRange, coordinator: coordinator)
46	        
47	        var needUpdate = false
48	        if coordinator.cachedAlarms != alarms {
49	            coordinator.cachedAlarms = alarms
50	            uiView.rightAxis.removeAllLimitLines()
51	            drawLines(chart: uiView)
52	            needUpdate = true
53	        }
54	        
55	        if updateMoving || needUpdate {
56	            uiView.notifyDataSetChanged()
57	        }
58	    }
59	    
60	    func makeCoordinator() -> RadiationLevelChartCoordinator {
61	        RadiationLevelChartCoordinator(scale: $scale)
62	    }
63	
64	    private func drawLines(chart: LineChartView) {
65	        if let lvl1 = alarms?.level1 {
66	            chart.rightAxis.addLimitLine(limitLine(color: .accentYellow, value: lvl1))
67	        }
68	        
69	        if let lvl2 = alarms?.level2 {
70	            chart.rightAxis.addLimitLine(limitLine(color: .accentRed, value: lvl2))
71	        }
72	    }
73	    
74	    private func limitLine(color: Color, value: Decimal) -> ChartLimitLine {
75	        let limitLine = ChartLimitLine(limit: value.doubleValue)
76	        limitLine.lineColor = NSUIColor(color)
77	        limitLine.lineWidth = 0.5
78	        return limitLine
79	    }
80	}
81	
82	#Preview {
83	    let date = Date.now
84	    let rawData: [RadiationLevelChartDataModel] = [
85	        .init(count: 25.5, time: date.timeIntervalSince1970),
86	        .init(count: 5.4, time: date.addingTimeInterval(-20).timeIntervalSince1970),
87	        .init(count: 15.4, time: date.addingTimeInterval(-30).timeIntervalSince1970),
88	        .init(count: 5.4, time: date.addingTimeInterval(-40).timeIntervalSince1970)
89	    ]
90	    let purifiedData: [RadiationLevelChartDataModel] = [
91	        .init(count: 5.4, time: date.addingTimeInterval(-50).timeIntervalSince1970),
92	        .init(count: 25.5, time: date.addingTimeInterval(-120).timeIntervalSince1970),
93	        .init(count: 15.5, time: date.addingTimeInterval(-55).timeIntervalSince1970)
94	    ]
95	    
96	    RadiationLevelChartView(
97	        rawData: rawData,
98	        purifiedData: purifiedData,
99	        timeRange: .minute,
100	        scale: .constant(.initial),
101	        radiationType: .countRate,
102	        alarms: .init(radiationType: .radiationLevel(.countRate), level1: 10, level2: 20),
103	        mainSourceForCalculating: true,
104	        indexes: .constant([])
105	    )
106	}
107

1	//
2	//  LineChartView+FocusRect.swift
3	//  RadiaCode
4	//
5	//  Created by Matvey Kozin on 12.11.2024.
6	//
7	
8	import DGCharts
9	import UIKit
10	import SwiftUI
11	
12	extension LineChartView {
13	    
14	    enum Consts {
15	        static let rawDataSetLabel = "rawDataSet"
16	        static let purifiedDataSetLabel = "purifiedDataSet"
17	    }
18	    
19	    static func makeChartView(
20	        rawData: [RadiationLevelChartDataModel],
21	        purifiedData: [RadiationLevelChartDataModel],
22	        lineWidth: CGFloat,
23	        color: NSUIColor
24	    ) -> LineChartView {
25	        let chartView = LineChartView()
26	        let rawDataSet = TimeOrderedLineDataSet(label: Consts.rawDataSetLabel)
27	        rawDataSet.axisDependency = .right
28	        rawData.forEach { data in
29	            rawDataSet.addEntryOrdered(ChartDataEntry(x: data.time, y: Double(data.count)))
30	        }
31	        rawDataSet.setColor(NSUIColor(.light02))
32	        rawDataSet.lineWidth = lineWidth
33	
34	        rawDataSet.drawCirclesEnabled = false
35	        rawDataSet.drawValuesEnabled = false
36	        rawDataSet.highlightEnabled = false
37	        rawDataSet.setDrawHighlightIndicators(false)
38	
39	        let purifiedDataSet = TimeOrderedLineDataSet(label: Consts.purifiedDataSetLabel)
40	        purifiedDataSet.axisDependency = .right
41	        purifiedData.forEach { data in
42	            purifiedDataSet.addEntryOrdered(ChartDataEntry(x: data.time, y: Double(data.count)))
43	        }
44	        purifiedDataSet.setColor(color)
45	        purifiedDataSet.lineWidth = 1
46	        purifiedDataSet.fillAlpha = 0.25
47	        purifiedDataSet.fill = ColorFill(color: color)
48	        purifiedDataSet.drawFilledEnabled = true
49	        purifiedDataSet.drawCirclesEnabled = false
50	        purifiedDataSet.drawValuesEnabled = false
51	        purifiedDataSet.highlightEnabled = false
52	        purifiedDataSet.setDrawHighlightIndicators(false)
53	        
54	        let data = LineChartData(dataSets: [rawDataSet, purifiedDataSet])
55	        chartView.data = data
56	        
57	        chartView.dragYEnabled = false
58	        chartView.scaleYEnabled = false
59	        chartView.highlightPerDragEnabled = false
60	        chartView.doubleTapToZoomEnabled = false
61	        chartView.leftAxis.enabled = false
62	        chartView.legend.enabled = false
63	        chartView.autoScaleMinMaxEnabled = true
64	        chartView.minOffset = 0
65	        
66	        return chartView
67	    }
68	    
69	    static func makeRadiationLevelChart(
70	        rawData: [RadiationLevelChartDataModel],
71	        purifiedData: [RadiationLevelChartDataModel],
72	        timeRange: RadiationLevelOptions.TimeRange,
73	        scale: RadiationLevelOptions.Scale,
74	        color: NSUIColor,
75	        coordinator: RadiationLevelChartCoordinator,
76	        indexes: Binding<[Int]>,
77	        mainSourceForCalculating: Bool
78	    ) -> LineChartView {
79	        let chartView = makeChartView(
80	            rawData: rawData,
81	            purifiedData: purifiedData,
82	            lineWidth: 1,
83	            color: color
84	        )
85	        chartView.delegate = coordinator
86	
87	        chartView.pinchZoomEnabled = true
88	        chartView.dragXEnabled = true
89	        
90	        chartView.xAxis.labelTextColor = UIColor(.textSecondary)
91	        chartView.xAxis.labelPosition = .bottom
92	        chartView.xAxis.valueFormatter = DateAxisValueFormatter()
93	        chartView.xAxis.gridLineDashPhase = 4.0
94	        chartView.xAxis.gridLineDashLengths = [4.0]
95	        chartView.xAxis.axisLineColor = .clear
96	        chartView.xAxis.gridColor = UIColor(.separatorFill)
97	        chartView.xAxis.labelCount = 4
98	        chartView.xAxis.drawGridLinesBehindDataEnabled = true
99	
100	        chartView.rightAxis.valueFormatter = PrecisionAxisValueFormatter()
101	        chartView.rightAxis.axisMinimum = 0
102	
103	        chartView.rightAxis.axisLineColor = .clear
104	        chartView.rightAxis.labelTextColor = UIColor(.textSecondary)
105	        chartView.rightAxis.labelPosition = .insideChart
106	        chartView.rightAxis.labelXOffset = 32
107	        chartView.extraRightOffset = 32
108	        chartView.rightAxis.gridColor = UIColor(.separatorFill)
109	        
110	        chartView.renderer = RadiationLevelChartRenderer(
111	            dataProvider: chartView,
112	            animator: chartView.chartAnimator,
113	            viewPortHandler: chartView.viewPortHandler,
114	            indexes: indexes,
115	            mainSourceForCalculating: mainSourceForCalculating
116	        )
117	
118	        chartView.legendRenderer = NoOpLegendRenderer(
119	            viewPortHandler: chartView.viewPortHandler,
120	            legend: chartView.legend
121	        )
122	
123	        chartView.leftYAxisRenderer = NoOpYAxisRenderer(
124	            viewPortHandler: chartView.viewPortHandler,
125	            axis: chartView.leftAxis,
126	            transformer: chartView.getTransformer(forAxis: .left)
127	        )
128	        
129	        switch scale {
130	        case .initial:
131	            if let data = chartView.data {
132	                chartView.focusX(minXValue: data.xMax - timeRange.timeInterval, maxXValue: data.xMax)
133	            }
134	        case let .live(minX, maxX),
135	            let .position(minX, maxX):
136	            chartView.focusX(minXValue: minX, maxXValue: maxX)
137	        }
138	        
139	        Task { @MainActor in
140	            chartView.notifyDataSetChanged()
141	        }
142	        
143	        coordinator.cachedPurifiedData = purifiedData
144	        coordinator.cachedRawData = rawData
145	        coordinator.cachedTimeRange = timeRange
146	        coordinator.cachedScale = scale
147	        
148	        return chartView
149	    }
150	    
151	    @discardableResult
152	    func updateData(
153	        rawData: [RadiationLevelChartDataModel],
154	        purifiedData: [RadiationLevelChartDataModel],
155	        coordinator: RadiationLevelCoordinatorProtocol
156	    ) -> Bool {
157	        guard
158	            let rawDataSet = data?.dataSets.first(where: { $0.label == Consts.rawDataSetLabel }) as? LineChartDataSet,
159	            let purifiedDataSet = data?.dataSets.first(where: { $0.label == Consts.purifiedDataSetLabel }) as? LineChartDataSet
160	        else {
161	            return false
162	        }
163	       
164	        var changed = false
165	
166	        if rawDataSet.count > RadiationLevelViewModel.Consts.rawDataMaxCount {
167	            rawDataSet.removeFirst(rawDataSet.count - RadiationLevelViewModel.Consts.rawDataMaxCount)
168	            changed = true
169	        }
170	        
171	        if rawData != coordinator.cachedRawData {
172	            if rawData.isEmpty {
173	                rawDataSet.removeAll(keepingCapacity: true)
174	            } else {
175	                let excludeArray = Set(coordinator.cachedRawData)
176	                let addedObjects = rawData.filter { !excludeArray.contains($0) }
177	                for addedObject in addedObjects {
178	                    let chartDataEntry: ChartDataEntry = .init(x: addedObject.time, y: Double(addedObject.count))
179	                    _ = rawDataSet.addEntryOrdered(chartDataEntry)
180	                }
181	            }
182	            coordinator.cachedRawData = rawData
183	            changed = true
184	        }
185	        
186	        if purifiedData != coordinator.cachedPurifiedData {
187	            if purifiedData.isEmpty {
188	                purifiedDataSet.removeAll(keepingCapacity: true)
189	            } else {
190	                let excludeArray = Set(coordinator.cachedPurifiedData)
191	                let addedObjects = purifiedData.filter { !excludeArray.contains($0) }
192	                for addedObject in addedObjects {
193	                    let chartDataEntry: ChartDataEntry = .init(x: addedObject.time, y: Double(addedObject.count))
194	                    _ = purifiedDataSet.addEntryOrdered(chartDataEntry)
195	                }
196	            }
197	            coordinator.cachedPurifiedData = purifiedData
198	            changed = true
199	        }
200	        
201	        return changed
202	    }
203	
204	    func updateMoving(
205	        scale: RadiationLevelOptions.Scale,
206	        timeRange: RadiationLevelOptions.TimeRange,
207	        coordinator: RadiationLevelCoordinatorMovingProtocol
208	    ) -> Bool {
209	        guard let data else { return false }
210	
211	        var needUpdate = false
212	
213	        var minXValue: Double? = nil
214	        var maxXValue: Double? = nil
215	        
216	        var isReseted = false
217	        if coordinator.cachedScale != scale {
218	            coordinator.cachedScale = scale
219	            switch scale {
220	            case .initial:
221	                isReseted = true
222	                minXValue = data.xMax - timeRange.timeInterval
223	                maxXValue = data.xMax
224	            case let .live(minX, maxX),
225	                let .position(minX, maxX):
226	                minXValue = minX
227	                maxXValue = maxX
228	                needUpdate = true
229	            }
230	        }
231	        
232	        if timeRange != coordinator.cachedTimeRange {
233	            coordinator.cachedTimeRange = timeRange
234	            if !isReseted {
235	                minXValue = data.xMax - timeRange.timeInterval
236	                maxXValue = data.xMax
237	            }
238	        }
239	        
240	        if let minXValue, let maxXValue {
241	            focusX(minXValue: minXValue, maxXValue: maxXValue)
242	        }
243	        
244	        return scale.isAutoMoving || needUpdate
245	    }
246	}
247	
248	#Preview {
249	    let date = Date.now
250	    let rawData: [RadiationLevelChartDataModel] = [
251	        .init(count: 25.5, time: date.timeIntervalSince1970),
252	        .init(count: 5.4, time: date.addingTimeInterval(-20).timeIntervalSince1970),
253	        .init(count: 15.4, time: date.addingTimeInterval(-30).timeIntervalSince1970),
254	        .init(count: 5.4, time: date.addingTimeInterval(-40).timeIntervalSince1970)
255	    ]
256	    let purifiedData: [RadiationLevelChartDataModel] = [
257	        .init(count: 5.4, time: date.addingTimeInterval(-50).timeIntervalSince1970),
258	        .init(count: 25.5, time: date.addingTimeInterval(-120).timeIntervalSince1970),
259	        .init(count: 15.5, time: date.addingTimeInterval(-55).timeIntervalSince1970)
260	    ]
261	
262	    LineChartView.makeRadiationLevelChart(
263	        rawData: rawData,
264	        purifiedData: purifiedData,
265	        timeRange: .minute,
266	        scale: .initial,
267	        color: NSUIColor(.accentPurple),
268	        coordinator: RadiationLevelChartCoordinator(scale: .constant(.initial)),
269	        indexes: .constant([0, 1, 2]),
270	        mainSourceForCalculating: true
271	    )
272	}
273

content.text: Perfect! Now let me check the RadiationLevelOverlayView as well:

content.content:
1	//
2	//  RadiationLevelOverlayView.swift
3	//  Radiacode
4	//
5	//  Created by Matvey Kozin on 12.02.2024.
6	//
7	
8	import SwiftUI
9	
10	struct RadiationLevelOverlayView: View {
11	    
12	    let radiationType: RadiationType.Level
13	    let value: Float?
14	    let error: Float?
15	    let level: SignalizationAlarmLevel
16	    
17	    var body: some View {
18	        RadiationTypeLabelView(value: value, radiationType: .radiationLevel(radiationType))
19	            .label { value, unit in
20	                HStack(alignment: .top) {
21	                    VStack(alignment: .leading, spacing: 3) {
22	                        Text(radiationType.title)
23	                            .font(.system(size: 17, weight: .semibold))
24	                        Text(unit)
25	                            .foregroundStyle(.textSecondary)
26	                            .font(.system(size: 13))
27	                    }
28	                    .padding(.top, 5)
29	                    .padding(.leading, 8)
30	                    
31	                    Spacer()
32	                    VStack(alignment: .trailing) {
33	                        Text(value)
34	                            .font(.system(size: 38, weight: .bold, design: .rounded))
35	                            .foregroundStyle(level.color)
36	                        if let error {
37	                            Text(verbatim: "±\(String(format: "%.1f", error))%")
38	                                .font(.system(size: 13))
39	                                .foregroundStyle(.textPrimary)
40	                        }
41	                    }
42	                    .padding(.trailing, 42)
43	                }
44	            }
45	    }
46	}
47	
48	#Preview {
49	    VStack {
50	        RadiationLevelOverlayView(
51	            radiationType: .countRate,
52	            value: 12.3,
53	            error: 2.0,
54	            level: .level1
55	        )
56	        
57	        RadiationLevelOverlayView(
58	            radiationType: .countRate,
